3-broad.raw_response:
Based on my exploration: the server stores ECGs as Procedure (SNOMED 29303009) with no normal/abnormal qualifier. I'll use Playbook 10 (negation) — keep all ECG patients, subtract those with heart disease, interfering meds, or abnormal electrolytes.

Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct|29303009
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|53741008,http://snomed.info/sct|22298006,http://snomed.info/sct|84114007,http://snomed.info/sct|49436004,http://snomed.info/sct|63467002,http://snomed.info/sct|59118001,http://snomed.info/sct|56265001,http://snomed.info/sct|413838009,http://snomed.info/sct|194828000,http://snomed.info/sct|399211009,http://snomed.info/sct|49601007
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|3407,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1202,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|6918,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10582,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|8787,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2598
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2951-2&value-quantity=lt135|http://unitsofmeasure.org|mmol/L,gt145|http://unitsofmeasure.org|mmol/L
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2823-3&value-quantity=lt3.5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|mmol/L,gt5.0|http://unitsofmeasure.org|mmol/L

3-expert.raw_response:
Server has only Condition (cardiac dx) and Procedure (ECG) data — no Observations or MedicationRequests. Best feasible negation query:

Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct|29303009
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|9651007,http://snomed.info/sct|63467002,http://snomed.info/sct|59118001,http://snomed.info/sct|233917008,http://snomed.info/sct|56265001

1-naive.prompt_text: Find patients with normal electrocardiogram readings who do not have heart disease, are not taking interfering medications, and have normal electrolyte levels at the time of the ECG.

1-expert.prompt_text: Find patients with normal electrocardiogram readings who do not have heart disease, are not taking interfering medications, and have normal electrolyte levels at the time of the ECG.

1-naive.raw_response:
```
# ── 1. NORMAL ECG OBSERVATIONS ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# 12-lead ECG study with Normal interpretation
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|11524-6&interpretation=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/v3-ObservationInterpretation|N&status=final&_include=Observation:subject

# 12-lead ECG panel with Normal interpretation
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|34534-8&interpretation=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/v3-ObservationInterpretation|N&status=final&_include=Observation:subject

# EKG impression coded as Normal ECG finding (SNOMED)
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|8601-7&value-concept=http://snomed.info/sct|17331003&status=final&_include=Observation:subject

# ── 2. HEART DISEASE CONDITIONS (patients to EXCLUDE) ────────────────────────
# General heart disease (SNOMED)
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|56265001&clinical-status=active

# Coronary artery disease
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I25.10&clinical-status=active

# Congestive heart failure
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I50.9&clinical-status=active

# Atrial fibrillation
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I48.91&clinical-status=active

# Cardiomyopathy
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I42.9&clinical-status=active

# Acute myocardial infarction
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I21.9&clinical-status=active

# Valvular heart disease
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I35.1&clinical-status=active

# Congenital heart disease
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|Q24.9&clinical-status=active

# ── 3. INTERFERING MEDICATIONS (patients to EXCLUDE) ─────────────────────────
# Digoxin (causes characteristic ST changes)
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|3407&status=active

# Amiodarone (Class III – QT prolongation)
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|703&status=active

# Sotalol (Class III – QT prolongation)
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|9947&status=active

# Dofetilide (Class III – QT prolongation)
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|60793&status=active

# Quinidine (Class Ia – QRS/QT widening)
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|9071&status=active

# Procainamide (Class Ia)
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|8750&status=active

# Flecainide (Class Ic – QRS widening)
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|4013&status=active

# Propafenone (Class Ic)
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|8787&status=active

# Verapamil (CCB non-DHP – PR prolongation, bradycardia)
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|11170&status=active

# Diltiazem (CCB non-DHP – PR prolongation)
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|3443&status=active

# Amitriptyline (TCA – QRS/QT widening)
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|704&status=active

# Haloperidol (antipsychotic – QT prolongation)
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|5093&status=active

# Lithium (T-wave changes)
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|6448&status=active

# ── 4. NORMAL ELECTROLYTE LEVELS (concurrent with ECG date) ─────────────────
# Potassium – normal range 3.5–5.0 mEq/L
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2823-3&value-quantity=ge3.5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|meq/L&value-quantity=le5.0|http://unitsofmeasure.org|meq/L&status=final

# Sodium – normal range 136–145 mEq/L
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2951-2&value-quantity=ge136|http://unitsofmeasure.org|meq/L&value-quantity=le145|http://unitsofmeasure.org|meq/L&status=final

# Total Calcium – normal range 8.5–10.5 mg/dL
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|17861-6&value-quantity=ge8.5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|mg/dL&value-quantity=le10.5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|mg/dL&status=final

# Magnesium – normal range 1.7–2.2 mg/dL
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2601-3&value-quantity=ge1.7|http://unitsofmeasure.org|mg/dL&value-quantity=le2.2|http://unitsofmeasure.org|mg/dL&status=final
```

---

### How to use these queries together

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| **1** | Execute **Group 1** queries → collect patient IDs + ECG `effectiveDateTime` |
| **2** | Execute **Group 2** queries → remove any patient IDs present here (active heart disease) |
| **3** | Execute **Group 3** queries → remove any patient IDs present here (interfering medications active at ECG date) |
| **4** | Execute **Group 4** queries filtered with `date=[ECG date range ±24 h]` per patient → retain only patients with **all four** electrolytes in normal range coinciding with the ECG |

> ⚠️ **Temporal correlation** for electrolytes "at the time of the ECG" must be applied at the application layer by adding `&date=` range parameters to the Group 4 queries once ECG dates are known.
1-naive.prompt_text: Find patients with normal electrocardiogram readings who do not have heart disease, are not taking interfering medications, and have normal electrolyte levels at the time of the ECG.

1-broad.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries: Normal ECG Patients Without Heart Disease, Interfering Meds, or Electrolyte Imbalances

The following queries collectively identify the target population. Queries 2 and 3 are **exclusion queries** (patients found here should be removed from results).

---

### 🔷 1. Normal ECG Observations
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|11524-6,http://loinc.org|34534-8,http://loinc.org|8601-7&interpretation=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/v3-ObservationInterpretation|N&status=final&_include=Observation:subject
```

---

### 🔴 2. Heart Disease Conditions — **(Exclusion: patients WITH heart disease)**
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|56265001,http://snomed.info/sct|53741008,http://snomed.info/sct|84114007,http://snomed.info/sct|44984001,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I20,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I21,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I25,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I42,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I44,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I48,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I49,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I50&clinical-status=active&verification-status=confirmed&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### 🔴 3. ECG-Interfering Medications — **(Exclusion: patients ON interfering meds)**
*(Includes antiarrhythmics, digoxin, QT-prolonging agents)*
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|3407,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|703,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|9071,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|9947,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|4123,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|8638,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|3484,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41493&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
```

---

### 🔷 4. Normal Serum Potassium (3.5–5.0 mEq/L)
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2823-3&interpretation=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/v3-ObservationInterpretation|N&status=final&_include=Observation:subject
```
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2823-3&value-quantity=ge3.5||meq%2FL&value-quantity=le5.0||meq%2FL&status=final
```

---

### 🔷 5. Normal Serum Sodium (135–145 mEq/L)
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2951-2&interpretation=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/v3-ObservationInterpretation|N&status=final&_include=Observation:subject
```
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2951-2&value-quantity=ge135||meq%2FL&value-quantity=le145||meq%2FL&status=final
```

---

### 🔷 6. Normal Serum Calcium (8.5–10.5 mg/dL)
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|17861-6&interpretation=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/v3-ObservationInterpretation|N&status=final&_include=Observation:subject
```
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|17861-6&value-quantity=ge8.5||mg%2FdL&value-quantity=le10.5||mg%2FdL&status=final
```

---

### 🔷 7. Normal Serum Magnesium (1.7–2.2 mg/dL)
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|19123-9&interpretation=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/v3-ObservationInterpretation|N&status=final&_include=Observation:subject
```
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|19123-9&value-quantity=ge1.7||mg%2FdL&value-quantity=le2.2||mg%2FdL&status=final
```

---

### 🔷 8. Normal Serum Chloride (98–107 mEq/L)
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2075-0&interpretation=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/v3-ObservationInterpretation|N&status=final&_include=Observation:subject
```
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2075-0&value-quantity=ge98||meq%2FL&value-quantity=le107||meq%2FL&status=final
```

---

### 📋 Summary Table

| Query | Purpose | LOINC/Code Key |
|---|---|---|
| Q1 | Normal ECG findings | `11524-6`, `34534-8`, `8601-7` |
| Q2 | ❌ Exclude heart disease | ICD-10 I20–I50, SNOMED `56265001` |
| Q3 | ❌ Exclude digoxin, amiodarone, quinidine, sotalol, flecainide, procainamide | RxNorm codes |
| Q4–Q8 | Normal electrolytes (K⁺, Na⁺, Ca²⁺, Mg²⁺, Cl⁻) | LOINC panel |

> **Note:** Use `date` or `_lastUpdated` parameters to temporally align ECG and electrolyte observations (e.g., `date=ge2026-01-01`). Patients must appear in Q1 + Q4–Q8 **and NOT** in Q2 or Q3.

1-expert.raw_response:
# FHIR Queries: Normal ECG Patients (Multi-Criteria)

> **Note:** This complex clinical scenario requires multiple queries. Queries 1–2 identify qualifying patients; Queries 3–4 identify patients to **exclude** (heart disease, interfering meds); Queries 5–8 confirm normal electrolytes. Results must be cross-referenced programmatically.

---

## ✅ Query 1 — Normal ECG Observations (Interpretation-Based)
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|11524-6,http://loinc.org|11522-0,http://loinc.org|34534-8&interpretation=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/v3-ObservationInterpretation|N&_include=Observation:patient
```

## ✅ Query 2 — Normal ECG Observations (Coded Value — SNOMED)
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|8601-7,http://loinc.org|64110-0&value-concept=http://snomed.info/sct|17134009&_include=Observation:patient
```

---

## 🚫 Query 3 — Active Heart Disease Conditions (EXCLUSION — SNOMED)
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|56265001,http://snomed.info/sct|53741008,http://snomed.info/sct|22298006,http://snomed.info/sct|84114007,http://snomed.info/sct|49436004,http://snomed.info/sct|13213009&clinical-status=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-clinical|active&_include=Condition:patient
```

```

## 🚫 Query 5 — Active ECG-Interfering Medications (EXCLUSION)
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|3407,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|703,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|9054,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|9947,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|4441,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|8814,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|704,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|5093,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|6813&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

```

1-expert.prompt_text: Find patients with normal electrocardiogram readings who do not have heart disease, are not taking interfering medications, and have normal electrolyte levels at the time of the ECG.

2-expert.raw_response:
I have now verified all components:

**Server Data Map:**
| Component | Resource | Codes Found | Count |
|---|---|---|---|
| ECG procedure | `Procedure` | SNOMED `29303009` | **56** |
| Cardiac conditions (heart disease) | `Condition` | SNOMED `9651007` (Long QT), `63467002` (LBBB), `59118001` (RBBB), `233917008` (AV block) + broader heart disease | **67/70** |
| Electrolyte labs | `Observation` | LOINC `2951-2` (Na), `2823-3` (K), `2075-0` (Cl), `1963-8` (HCO3) | **0** — no lab data |
| Interfering medications | `MedicationRequest` / `MedicationStatement` | RxNorm antiarrhythmics/digoxin | **0** — no Rx data |

**Clinical reasoning applied:**
- **Normal ECG** → `Procedure` code `29303009` (Electrocardiographic procedure) — the only ECG code present; normality is encoded by absence of cardiac Conditions
- **No heart disease** → Exclusion of patients with Condition codes for Long QT, LBBB, RBBB, AV block, AFib, heart failure, MI, CAD (applied at application level — FHIR doesn't support NOT in URL queries)
- **No interfering medications** → No server data; expressed via `MedicationRequest` (returns 0)
- **Normal electrolytes** → Observation with LOINC codes for Na/K/Cl/HCO3 with physiological value-quantity bounds (returns 0 — no lab data on server)

---

Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct|29303009
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2951-2&value-quantity=ge135&value-quantity=le145
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2823-3&value-quantity=ge3.5&value-quantity=le5.0
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2075-0&value-quantity=ge98&value-quantity=le106
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|1963-8&value-quantity=ge22&value-quantity=le29
```

3-naive.raw_response:
I now have all the information needed. Let me compile the findings:

**Server topology confirmed:**
| Resource | Count | Code System | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Procedure` | 56 | SNOMED `29303009` | ALL are ECG procedures |
| `Condition` | 70 | SNOMED | Cardiac arrhythmias (BBBs, etc.) → 45 match heart-disease codes |
| `MedicationRequest` | 0 | — | No med data available |
| `Observation` | 0 | — | No lab data available |
| `DiagnosticReport` | 0 | — | Not available |

**Strategy (Playbooks 6 + 10 + 7 + 11):**
- **Line 1 — KEEP**: All ECG procedures (SNOMED `29303009`)
- **Line 2 — SUBTRACT**: Heart disease Conditions (SNOMED: heart failure, CAD, cardiomyopathy, arrhythmia, bundle-branch blocks, Afib, umbrella heart disease) → 45 records
- **Line 3 — SUBTRACT**: ECG-interfering MedicationRequests (digoxin, amiodarone, quinidine, sotalol, metoprolol, propranolol, diltiazem, verapamil — all verified RxNorm ingredient codes; returns 0 on this server, no-op subtract)
- **Lines 4–7 — KEEP**: Normal electrolyte Observations with `value-quantity` reference-range filters (K, Na, Mg, Ca; returns 0 on this server but included per complete phenotype specification)

Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|56265001,http://snomed.info/sct|84114007,http://snomed.info/sct|53741008,http://snomed.info/sct|85898001,http://snomed.info/sct|698247007,http://snomed.info/sct|49436004,http://snomed.info/sct|59118001,http://snomed.info/sct|63467002
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|3407,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|703,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|9068,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|9947,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|6918,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|8787,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|3443,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|11170
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2823-3&value-quantity=ge3.5||meq%2FL&value-quantity=le5.0||meq%2FL
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2951-2&value-quantity=ge136||meq%2FL&value-quantity=le145||meq%2FL
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2601-3&value-quantity=ge1.7||mg%2FdL&value-quantity=le2.2||mg%2FdL
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|17861-6&value-quantity=ge8.5||mg%2FdL&value-quantity=le10.2||mg%2FdL

### How the harness assembles this cohort

| # | Query | Role | What it enforces |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `Procedure` SNOMED `29303009` | **KEEP** | Patient had an ECG performed |
| 2 | `Condition` 7 cardiac SNOMED codes | **SUBTRACT** | No heart disease (heart failure, CAD, cardiomyopathy, arrhythmia, Afib, BBBs, umbrella heart disease) |
| 3 | `MedicationRequest` 8 RxNorm codes | **SUBTRACT** | Not on digoxin, amiodarone, quinidine, sotalol, metoprolol, propranolol, diltiazem, or verapamil |
| 4 | `Observation` K⁺ 3.5–5.0 mEq/L | **KEEP** | Normal potassium |

1-broad.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries — Electrocardiographic Procedures (Cardiac Conduction / QRS-Interval Studies)

```
Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct|29303009&_include=Procedure:patient&_include=Procedure:subject

Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct|164847006&_include=Procedure:patient&_include=Procedure:subject

Procedure?code=http://loinc.org|11524-6&_include=Procedure:patient&_include=Procedure:subject

Procedure?code=http://loinc.org|34534-8&_include=Procedure:patient&_include=Procedure:subject

Observation?code=http://loinc.org|11524-6&_include=Observation:patient&_include=Observation:subject

Observation?code=http://loinc.org|8625-5&_include=Observation:patient&_include=Observation:subject

Observation?code=http://loinc.org|8636-2&_include=Observation:patient&_include=Observation:subject

Observation?code=http://loinc.org|8637-0&_include=Observation:patient&_include=Observation:subject
```

---

### Code Reference

| Resource | System | Code | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Procedure` | SNOMED CT | `29303009` | Electrocardiographic procedure |
| `Procedure` | SNOMED CT | `164847006` | Standard 12-lead ECG |
| `Procedure` / `Observation` | LOINC | `11524-6` | EKG study |
| `Procedure` / `Observation` | LOINC | `34534-8` | EKG 12-channel panel |
| `Observation` | LOINC | `8625-5` | QRS duration (global) |
| `Observation` | LOINC | `8636-2` | QRS duration – Lead I |
| `Observation` | LOINC | `8637-0` | QRS duration – Lead II |

### Notes
- **`_include=Procedure:patient`** pulls associated `Patient` resources in a single request.
- For **pharmacogenetic study** cohorts, consider adding `ResearchSubject` or `ResearchStudy` filters (e.g., `ResearchSubject?study=<id>&_include=ResearchSubject:individual`) to scope to enrolled healthy controls.
- If your system stores ECGs as `DiagnosticReport`, add: `DiagnosticReport?code=http://loinc.org|11524-6&_include=DiagnosticReport:subject`

1-expert.raw_response:
## FHIR Query: Electrocardiographic Procedure (PheKB Cardiac Conduction / QRS-Interval Cohort)

```
Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct|29303009
```

---

### Query Rationale

| Parameter | Value | Notes |
|-----------|-------|-------|
| **Resource** | `Procedure` | Procedure-only phenotype — no Condition or MedicationRequest path |
| **Code System** | `http://snomed.info/sct` | SNOMED CT |
| **Concept Code** | `29303009` | *Electrocardiographic procedure* |
| **PheKB Algorithm** | Cohort identified by ECG performance | Healthy volunteers, not disease-coded patients |

---

### Recommended Enhancements

To retrieve the associated patient records directly, add `_include`:

```
Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct|29303009&_include=Procedure:patient
```

To filter for completed procedures only (recommended for PheKB cohort fidelity):

```
Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct|29303009&status=completed&_include=Procedure:patient
```

---

### Clinical Context
- **SNOMED CT `29303009`** — *Electrocardiographic procedure* is the canonical parent concept and will capture standard 12-lead ECGs as well as related ECG modalities, making it ideal for broad PheKB cohort identification.
- Per the PheKB algorithm, **no ICD-10 diagnosis codes** (e.g., conduction disorders) or **medication codes** are used as inclusion criteria — the ECG procedure record itself is the sole defining criterion for this pharmacogenomics study population.
